title: Address sanitizer fails on verilated_trace_imp.h - load/store to misaligned address
body:
Verilator version
Verilator 5.022 2024-02-24 rev v5.020-157-g2b4852048

OS
WSL2 - Ubuntu22.04 - 5.15.133.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2

I tried to add the argument "--runtime-debug" to "t_trace_cat_fst" from "test_regress" (it would enable the sanitizer), and it gave the results:
```
driver: Leaving directory '/home/tikifire/source-code/verilator/verilator/test_regress/obj_vltmt/t_trace_cat_fst'
        obj_vltmt/t_trace_cat_fst/Vt_trace_cat_fst    > obj_vltmt/t_trace_cat_fst/vlt_sim.log
/home/tikifire/source-code/verilator/verilator/test_regress/../include/gtkwave/fstapi.c:1649:116: runtime error: left shift of negative value -2
/home/tikifire/source-code/verilator/verilator/test_regress/../include/verilated_trace_imp.h:577:69: runtime error: store to misaligned address 0x60d000000114 for type 'uint64_t', which requires 8 byte alignment
0x60d000000114: note: pointer points here
  0c 00 00 00 be be be be  be be be be be be be be  be be be be be be be be  be be be be be be be be
              ^
/home/tikifire/source-code/verilator/verilator/test_regress/../include/verilated_trace_imp.h:196:31: runtime error: load of misaligned address 0x60d000000114 for type 'const uint64_t', which requires 8 byte alignment
0x60d000000114: note: pointer points here
  0c 00 00 00 01 00 00 00  00 00 00 00 0d 00 00 00  c0 02 00 00 70 60 00 00  00 00 00 00 02 00 00 00
              ^
*-* All Finished *-*
```

Actually, I noticed a similar issue - https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/3651
